Name: Description
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: A guided Greek mythology curriculum for families and co-ops who want stories, maps, art, narration, and writing to hang together as one coherent path.

Name: Publisher
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Chief Clanker

Name: Grade Level
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Elementary

Name: Link
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: https://lessonhollow.com/curriculum/greek-myths

Name: Title
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Description

Name: Read short biographies of 12 Greek gods and goddesses
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read "The Olympians" by Leonard Everett Fischer to start learning the names and roles of the Olympian gods and goddesses

Name: Watch a short overview of the Greek pantheon
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Get the big picture of the major gods from "The Life Guide" on YouTube before the details pile up

Name: Draw a map of places that host Greek mythology
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Make a simple labeled map so the world of the myths has places and structure

Name: Buy and play a card game related to Greek mythology
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Many card games incorporate the characters of Greek myths. Research the available games, choose one, and play it.

Name: Create your own god and goddess trading cards
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Make trading cards for your favorite Olympians with a picture of the god or goddess on one side and key facts about them on the other side.

Name: Read "The Battle of the Olympians and the Titans" by Cari Meister
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Learn the struggle that shaped the world before the heroes arrive

Name: Tell the Titan war as a campfire story
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Explain the conflict aloud as if you were telling it to friends around a campfire

Name: Read "Cronus the Titan Tells All" by Eric Braun
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: See the Titan war from a strong character voice

Name: Compare two versions of the Titan story
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Notice and discuss what changes when the same myth is told from a different point of view

Name: Read "Persephone" by Warwick Hutton
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read a strong picture-book telling of Persephone and the underworld

Name: Read The Pomegranate Seeds
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Compare another telling of Persephone and notice what details or tone shift

Name: Compare two Persephone retellings
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Write or discuss what stayed the same and what changed across versions

Name: Make a seasonal wheel for Persephone
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Show how the Persephone story explains the cycle of the seasons

Name: Read The Story of Persephone by Penelope Farmer
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Add one more concise retelling and notice what details repeat across versions

Name: Read The Story of Arachne
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Meet a myth about pride skill and transformation

Name: Illustrate the moment of transformation
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Draw the turning point in the Arachne story and label the emotion of the scene

Name: Read The Story of King Midas
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read the Midas story and trace how wish fulfillment becomes a curse

Name: Write a warning from King Midas
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Write a short message King Midas would send to someone tempted by greed

Name: Read The Story of Pygmalion
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read a myth about art longing and the line between making and loving

Name: Discuss what makes a good gift from the gods
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Use Pygmalion and Midas to compare blessing desire and consequence

Name: Read Icarus The Boy Who Flew
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read the core Icarus story in picture-book form

Name: Read Daedalus and Icarus by Penelope Farmer
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Compare another version of the same myth and track what each one emphasizes

Name: Read Icarus by M. S. Mason
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Add a third version and compare the emotional tone of the fall

Name: Compare three Icarus tellings
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Write or narrate what each retelling seems to think the lesson really is

Name: Design safer wings for escape
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Sketch a better escape plan and explain what Daedalus should have changed

Name: Read Theseus and the Road to Athens
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Start the hero arc of Theseus before the labyrinth

Name: Read Theseus and the Minotaur
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Follow Theseus into the labyrinth and track the choices that matter most

Name: Read A-maze-ing Minotaur
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Use a playful retelling to reinforce the central monster story

Name: Build a labyrinth map
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Make a visual path through the labyrinth and mark the turning points of the story

Name: Write Ariadne's advice to Theseus
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Write a short note from Ariadne that explains what really matters in the maze

Name: Read Medusa's Stony Stare
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read a focused version of the Medusa story and note what makes the monster memorable

Name: Compare monster myths
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Discuss how Medusa and the Minotaur are alike and how they are different

Name: Read The Flying Horse The Story of Pegasus
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Meet Pegasus and track how a creature becomes part of the heroic world

Name: Read The Story of Pegasus
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Compare another Pegasus retelling and note differences in tone or detail

Name: Read Jason and the Argonauts by Robert Byrd
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Begin the Jason story with a richly illustrated retelling

Name: Read Jason and the Golden Fleece
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Compare a second version and focus on the quest structure

Name: Read Jason and the Argonauts by H. H. Cardigan
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Use a third version to notice what stays central in the quest narrative

Name: Read Medea Tells All
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Shift to Medea's voice and see how point of view changes the Jason story

Name: Make an Argonaut crew chart
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: List the crew members gifts and jobs so the quest feels like a coordinated expedition

Name: Read The Trojan Horse
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Study the Trojan Horse as a myth of strategy disguise and consequence

Name: Debate whether the horse was clever or dishonorable
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Use evidence from the story to argue both sides before choosing your view

Name: Read The Story of Baucis and Philemon
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Read a smaller hospitality myth that shows how the gods judge human behavior

Name: Read The Story of Cadmus
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Follow the mythic founding story of Cadmus and trace how one act changes a whole city

Name: Look for patterns across the myths
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Identify repeating themes like pride hospitality trickery transformation and fate

Name: Choose your favorite myth and retell it as a final project
Greek Myths Through Stories, Maps, and R: Create a final retelling in writing art comic or oral storytelling form
